---
title: "Nextflow Workflow for Plant Whole-Genome Sequencing and Variant Calling"
author: "Mohamed Hussein"
date: "03-03-2026"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    mainfont: "Times New Roman"
    fontsize: 12pt
execute:
  eval: false
---

```         
 
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Tools Used in This Study

The following bioinformatics tools were used to build the variant calling workflow.

| Tool | Function |
|---------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
| Nextflow | Workflow management system used to orchestrate the pipeline |
| Conda | Environment manager for installing bioinformatics software |
| BWA | Align sequencing reads to the reference genome |
| Samtools | Manipulate and process SAM/BAM alignment files |
| FastQC | Assess sequencing read quality |
| fastp | Perform read trimming and quality filtering |
| Picard | Detect and remove PCR duplicates |
| BCFtools | Variant calling and variant filtering |
| Ensembl Plants VEP | Functional annotation of genomic variants |

These tools were integrated into a reproducible analysis workflow.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Environment Setup

Before running the workflow, the required software tools were installed.

## Install Conda

``` bash
wget https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh
bash Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh
```

Activate Conda:

``` bash
source ~/.bashrc
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Install Nextflow

``` bash
curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash
mv nextflow ~/bin/
```

Check installation:

``` bash
nextflow -version
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Install Bioinformatics Tools

The required tools were installed using Conda.

``` bash
conda create -n variant_pipeline \
bwa samtools bcftools fastqc fastp picard \
-c bioconda -c conda-forge
```

Activate environment:

``` bash
conda activate variant_pipeline
```

All tools were successfully installed and ready to run the workflow.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Workflow Execution

The pipeline was executed using the Nextflow workflow script.

``` bash
nextflow run main.nf -resume
```

Nextflow automatically executed each process sequentially:

-   reference download
-   genome indexing
-   sequencing read preprocessing
-   alignment
-   duplicate removal
-   variant calling

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Project Structure

To ensure reproducibility and proper workflow execution, the project follows a structured directory layout.

``` markdown
Bioinformatics-Analysis-Workflow/
│
├── envs/
│   └── plant_wgs_env.yml
│
├── notebooks/
│   └── Nextflow_plant_wgs_variant_calling_report.qmd
│
├── reports/
│   └── Nextflow_plant_wgs_variant_calling_report.pdf
│
├── figures/
│   └── vep_results.png
│
├── main.nf
├── nextflow.config
│
└── scripts/
```

## Directory Description

### envs/

Contains Conda environment configuration files required to run the workflow.

Example:

```         
plant_wgs_env.yml
```

This file ensures reproducibility of software dependencies.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### notebooks/

Contains analysis notebooks and Quarto report source files.

```         
Nextflow_plant_wgs_variant_calling_report.qmd
```

This file generates the final PDF report.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### reports/

Contains compiled reports generated from the notebooks.

```         
Nextflow_plant_wgs_variant_calling_report.pdf
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### figures/

Stores images used in the report.

Example:

```         
vep_results.png
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### main.nf

Main **Nextflow workflow script** defining pipeline processes such as:

-   reference download
-   indexing
-   read preprocessing
-   alignment
-   variant calling

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### nextflow.config

Defines workflow configuration including:

-   execution parameters
-   computing resources
-   environment settings

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### scripts/

Optional directory containing helper scripts used by pipeline processes.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Introduction

Whole-genome sequencing (WGS) enables comprehensive detection of genomic variants across an organism's genome.

In this analysis, we implemented a **reproducible Nextflow workflow** to process plant sequencing data and identify genomic variants.

The workflow includes:

1.  Reference genome acquisition
2.  Genome indexing
3.  Sequencing read download
4.  Quality control and trimming
5.  Read alignment
6.  Duplicate removal
7.  Variant calling
8.  Variant filtering and SNP extraction
9.  Functional annotation using Ensembl Plants VEP

The reference genome used in this study is **Arabidopsis thaliana TAIR10**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Workflow Overview

The workflow was implemented using **Nextflow** to ensure reproducibility and portability across computing environments.

``` bash
nextflow run main.nf -c nextflow.config -resume
```

The workflow consists of seven processes:

-   download_reference
-   index_reference
-   download_reads
-   qc_trimming
-   alignment
-   deduplication
-   variant_calling

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reference Genome Download

## Objective

Download the **Arabidopsis thaliana reference genome and annotation** from NCBI.

## Code

``` bash
datasets download genome accession GCF_000001735.4 --include genome,gff3
unzip ncbi_dataset.zip
```

## Results

Reference genome files were downloaded successfully.

```         
GCF_000001735.4_TAIR10.1_genomic.fna
genomic.gff
```

These files provide the **reference sequence and genome annotations** required for downstream alignment and variant interpretation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reference Genome Indexing

## Objective

Prepare the genome for efficient read alignment using BWA and Samtools.

## Code

``` bash
bwa index GCF_000001735.4_TAIR10.1_genomic.fna samtools faidx GCF_000001735.4_TAIR10.1_genomic.fna
```

## Results

The indexing process generated several files:

```         
.fna.amb 
.fna.ann 
.fna.bwt 
.fna.pac 
.fna.sa 
.fna.fai
```

These files enable **fast alignment of sequencing reads** to the reference genome.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Sequencing Read Download

## Objective

Download sequencing reads from the Sequence Read Archive (SRA).

## Code

``` bash
prefetch ERR11436045
fasterq-dump ERR11436045 --split-files
```

## Results

Paired-end FASTQ files were generated:

```         
ERR11436045_1.fastq
ERR11436045_2.fastq
```

These files contain the raw sequencing reads used for genome alignment.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Quality Control and Read Trimming

## Objective

Assess sequencing quality and remove low-quality bases.

## Code

``` bash
fastqc ERR11436045_1.fastq ERR11436045_2.fastq

fastp \
-i ERR11436045_1.fastq \
-I ERR11436045_2.fastq \
-o ERR11436045_1.trimmed.fastq \
-O ERR11436045_2.trimmed.fastq \
-l 100
```

## Results

Quality reports generated:

```         
fastp.html
fastp.json
fastqc_output/
```

Trimmed reads:

```         
ERR11436045_1.trimmed.fastq
ERR11436045_2.trimmed.fastq
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Read Alignment

## Objective

Align reads to the reference genome.

## Code

``` bash
bwa mem index/GCF_000001735.4_TAIR10.1_genomic.fna \
ERR11436045_1.trimmed.fastq \
ERR11436045_2.trimmed.fastq \
> aligned.sam
```

Convert SAM to BAM and sort:

``` bash
samtools view -bS aligned.sam > aligned.bam
samtools sort aligned.bam -o sorted.bam
samtools index sorted.bam
```

## Results

Example alignment statistics:

```         
Processed 531560 reads
mean insert size ≈ 330 bp
median insert size ≈ 355 bp
```

Generated files:

```         
ERR11436045_sorted_trimmed_ref_aligned.bam
ERR11436045_sorted_trimmed_ref_aligned.bam.bai
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Duplicate Removal

## Objective

Remove PCR duplicates.

## Code

``` bash
picard MarkDuplicates \
I=ERR11436045_sorted_trimmed_ref_aligned.bam \
O=ERR11436045_dedup.bam \
M=dup_metrics.txt
```

## Results

Output:

```         
ERR11436045_dedup.bam
dup_metrics.txt
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Variant Calling

## Objective

Detect genomic variants using bcftools.

## Code

``` bash
bcftools mpileup \
-f GCF_000001735.4_TAIR10.1_genomic.fna \
ERR11436045_dedup.bam \
-Ou | bcftools call -mv -Oz -o variants.vcf.gz
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Variant Filtering

``` bash
bcftools filter -i 'QUAL>30' variants.vcf.gz \
-Oz -o filtered_variants.vcf.gz
```

Extract SNPs:

``` bash
bcftools view -v snps filtered_variants.vcf.gz \
-Oz -o snps.vcf.gz
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Functional Annotation

Variant annotation was performed using **Ensembl Plants VEP**.

Annotation summary:

```         
Variants processed: 1,048,016
Novel variants: 17.9%
Known variants: 82.1%
Genes affected: 32,449
Regulatory regions: 53,563
```

Variant consequences:

```         
upstream_gene_variant ~40%
downstream_gene_variant ~30%
intron_variant ~6%
intergenic_variant ~4%
missense_variant ~3%
synonymous_variant ~3%
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Variant Annotation Results Visualization

![Variant annotation results](vep_results.png)

**Figure 1:** Distribution of variant consequences detected in the dataset based on Ensembl Plants VEP annotation.\
The figure summarizes the proportions of upstream, downstream, intronic, intergenic, missense, and synonymous variants (Functional Annotation section).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Conclusion

This study implemented a **reproducible workflow for plant WGS variant analysis**.

Key outcomes:

-   Successful alignment of sequencing reads
-   Detection of over **1 million genomic variants**
-   Functional annotation using Ensembl Plants VEP

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Reproducibility

Workflow manager:

```         
Nextflow
```

Environment manager:

```         
Conda
```

Key tools:

```         
BWA Samtools BCFtools FastQC fastp Picard
```

-   This workflow provides a fully reproducible and modular pipeline for plant whole-genome sequencing variant analysis, enabling consistent results and facilitating future genomic studies.
